free_text: Low-energy neutron fission cross section of Pu-241

MTR fast chopper
Each chamber contained a 2x3-
in. foil on which was deposited the fissile material
(80.2% Pu-241 with contaminant of 8.2% Pu-239) by a
painting and firing technique.
Time-of-flight. 8.5-m flight path
Gas scintillation detector
from the shapes of the total and fission cross section
curves shown in Figs. 1 and 2 (Phys.Rev.133,B390,1964)
it is evident that the broad 6-eV resonance is
primarily due to fission. From the resonance parameters
given by Simpson and Moore (Phys.Rev.123,5591961), the
scattering and radiative capture cross sections of
14+4 and 14+7 b, respectively, were calculated at 6 eV,
using the multilevel formula. Subtracting the
calculated value of sig(n,n), and sig(n,gamma), from
the measured total cross section of 434+-6 b, one
obtains a fission cross section of 406+-10 b.
